ucs.title: [LLM] Axiom (Group 72) sticky-keys / accessibility-feature debugger backdoor (Hikit-style RDP foothold)
ucs.description: Hunts the IFEO 'Debugger' registry hijack of Windows accessibility binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe, narrator.exe, magnify.exe, displayswitch.exe, atbroker.exe) — a near-signature TTP for Axiom / Group 72 / Hikit operations. The hijack yields a SYSTEM-context cmd shell at the locked logon screen reachable over RDP, which is precisely how Axiom couples T1546.008 with their T1021.001 / T1078 / T1563.002 RDP-heavy lateral movement.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.action IN ("modified","created") AND Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\*" AND (Registry.registry_path="*\\sethc.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\utilman.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\osk.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\narrator.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\magnify.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\displayswitch.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\atbroker.exe*") AND Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger" AND Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*cmd.exe*","*powershell.exe*","*pwsh.exe*","*cscript.exe*","*wscript.exe*","*rundll32.exe*","*regsvr32.exe*") by host Registry.user Registry.process_id Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name registry_path registry_value_data
ucs.kql:
// Axiom / Group 72 - accessibility-feature IFEO debugger hijack (sticky-keys backdoor)
let AccessibilityBins = dynamic([@"\sethc.exe", @"\utilman.exe", @"\osk.exe", @"\narrator.exe", @"\magnify.exe", @"\displayswitch.exe", @"\atbroker.exe"]);
let ShellPayloads = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let RegistryHits =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
    | where RegistryKey has_any (AccessibilityBins)
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "Debugger" or RegistryValueName =~ "GlobalFlag" or RegistryValueName =~ "ReportingMode" or RegistryValueName =~ "MonitorProcess"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (ShellPayloads)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
// Live-fire confirmation: accessibility binary spawning a shell as SYSTEM (the trigger of the backdoor)
let ProcessHits =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ChildImage = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentIntegrity = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel,
              IsRemoteSession = AccountName;
union isfuzzy=true RegistryHits, ProcessHits
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Axiom / Group 72 - accessibility-feature IFEO debugger hijack on Sentinel telemetry
let AccessibilityBins = dynamic([@"\sethc.exe", @"\utilman.exe", @"\osk.exe", @"\narrator.exe", @"\magnify.exe", @"\displayswitch.exe", @"\atbroker.exe"]);
let ShellPayloads = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let RegistryHits =
    ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
    | where RegistryKey has_any (AccessibilityBins)
    | where RegistryValue in~ ("Debugger","GlobalFlag","ReportingMode","MonitorProcess")
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (ShellPayloads)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
              RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData;
// Same intent on raw Windows - Sysmon EID 13 / SecurityEvent 4657 fallback
let WinHits =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4657
    | where ObjectName has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
    | where ObjectName has_any (AccessibilityBins)
    | where ObjectValueName =~ "Debugger"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname = Computer, ActorUsername = Account,
              ActingProcessName = ProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine = CommandLine,
              RegistryKey = ObjectName, RegistryValue = ObjectValueName, RegistryValueData = "";
// Live-fire: accessibility binary parented a shell
let ProcessHits =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe")
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname = Computer, ActorUsername = Account,
              ActingProcessName = ParentProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine = CommandLine,
              RegistryKey = "", RegistryValue = "", RegistryValueData = NewProcessName;
union isfuzzy=true RegistryHits, WinHits, ProcessHits
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Accessibility Feature IFEO Debugger Hijack (Axiom / Group 72)
id: 5d5b1a8a-1f6f-4d6f-bf7a-axiomg72ifeo
status: experimental
description: Detects registry-based hijack of Windows accessibility binaries via Image File Execution Options Debugger value. Long-running tradecraft of Axiom (Group 72) for SYSTEM-context RDP-screen backdoors.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0001/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1546/008/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1546.008
  - attack.g0001
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Image File Execution Options\'
  selection_binary:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - '\sethc.exe'
      - '\utilman.exe'
      - '\osk.exe'
      - '\narrator.exe'
      - '\magnify.exe'
      - '\displayswitch.exe'
      - '\atbroker.exe'
  selection_value:
    TargetObject|endswith:
      - '\Debugger'
      - '\GlobalFlag'
      - '\MonitorProcess'
  selection_payload:
    Details|contains:
      - 'cmd.exe'
      - 'powershell.exe'
      - 'pwsh.exe'
      - 'cscript.exe'
      - 'wscript.exe'
      - 'rundll32.exe'
      - 'regsvr32.exe'
      - 'mshta.exe'
  condition: selection_path and selection_binary and (selection_value or selection_payload)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate AppCompat shims (rare, name-coincidence only)
  - Developer use of GFlags against accessibility binaries (very rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Accessibility-feature IFEO hijack is one of Axiom's documented signature persistence techniques (G0001 lists T1546.008) and historically appears in Hikit/Derusbi intrusion sets attributed to the cluster. Combined with their reliance on RDP (T1021.001) and RDP session hijacking (T1563.002), the SYSTEM-shell-at-the-logon-screen pattern is far more diagnostic of this actor than generic process detect
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Axiom RDP session hijacking via SYSTEM-launched tscon.exe (T1563.002)
ucs.description: Hunts privilege-elevated `tscon.exe /dest:` invocations that hijack another user's existing RDP session without supplying credentials. Axiom is one of the few state-aligned actors with documented use of T1563.002 in conjunction with their RDP-heavy lateral movement (T1021.001) and valid-accounts (T1078) playbook — the technique requires SYSTEM context, which is typically achieved via the sticky-keys backdoor or a service-creation trick they also favour.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="tscon.exe" AND Processes.process="*/dest:*" AND (Processes.user IN ("SYSTEM","NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM","LOCAL SYSTEM") OR Processes.parent_process_name IN ("services.exe","sc.exe","psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","sethc.exe","utilman.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name parent_process process
ucs.kql:
// Axiom (G0001) RDP session hijack - tscon.exe /dest from SYSTEM context
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "tscon.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/dest:\S+"
| where AccountName =~ "system"
   or InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel == "System"
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("services.exe","sc.exe","psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentIntegrity = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel,
          GrandparentImage = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Axiom (G0001) RDP session hijack - tscon.exe /dest from SYSTEM context (Sentinel)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\tscon.exe"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/dest:\S+"
| where SubjectUserSid == "S-1-5-18"
   or TokenElevationType == "%%1936"
   or ParentProcessName has_any ("services.exe","sc.exe","psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName, SubjectUserSid,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: RDP Session Hijack via SYSTEM-context tscon.exe (Axiom / Group 72)
id: 9e7c8b21-3a44-4e3a-bb1c-axiomg72tscon
status: experimental
description: Detects tscon.exe spawned with /dest: switch under SYSTEM context, hijacking an existing RDP session without credentials. Strongly associated with Axiom (G0001) RDP-centric tradecraft (T1563.002).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0001/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1563/002/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.t1563.002
  - attack.t1021.001
  - attack.g0001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\tscon.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/dest:'
  selection_priv:
    - User|contains:
        - 'SYSTEM'
        - 'AUTORITE NT'
        - 'AUTORIT\xc9 NT'
    - IntegrityLevel: 'System'
    - ParentImage|endswith:
        - '\services.exe'
        - '\sc.exe'
        - '\psexec.exe'
        - '\psexesvc.exe'
        - '\sethc.exe'
        - '\utilman.exe'
        - '\osk.exe'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_priv
falsepositives:
  - Some helpdesk / shadowing tools wrap tscon.exe under a SYSTEM service - baseline before alerting
level: high
ucs.rationale: RDP session hijacking with tscon under SYSTEM is rare in the wild — most red-team tooling uses Mimikatz / Rubeus instead. Axiom is explicitly attributed with T1563.002, and it pairs naturally with their sticky-keys backdoor (which yields the SYSTEM context required for credential-less hijack). Pivoting on SYSTEM-context tscon /dest catches a very narrow technique that legitimate admins almost neve
_sigma_issues:
yaml/sigma error: mapping values are not allowed in this context
  in "<unicode string>", line 4, column 50
